_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: Melbourne, FL — Mayor Alfrey + Council D1 Smith + D2 LaRusso + D3 Neuman + D4 Bassett. Evidence-based finalization for the LOCAL tier. Rosters verified current June 2026. Confidence = evidence_local where positions scored, roster_verified where research yielded only nulls. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable source; null where no evidence (no party-default inferences). Adds the v5.0 local categories (public_justice, refuse_state_overreach) missing from prior records. Melbourne council seats are officially non-partisan; partisan registration only set where HARD evidence exists.

set.office: Mayor of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · 2nd term, ends Nov 2028; current charter is 2 consecutive 4-year terms)

profile.background: Retired law enforcement (3 years Sanford PD, 12 years Melbourne PD — K-9, Street Crimes, Community Policing, FTO; retired 2012). Florida Certified Contractor, Home Inspector, Insurance Agent; small-business owner. Eau Gallie HS; Seminole State College of Florida; Miami Dade College. First elected to City Council District 5 in 2016 (served as Vice Mayor); elected Mayor November 2020; re-elected November 5, 2024 with 50.84% (19,142 votes) over former mayor Kathy Meehan (25.92%) and Hazel Buggs (23.24%). Sits on Melbourne Orlando International Airport Authority Board and Melbourne Affordable Housing Board.

paul-alfrey.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Mayor of Melbourne, FL (2nd term to Nov 2028); not on 2026 ballot. Party R (Republican Mayors Association affiliate publicly recognized 2024 re-election). HARD evidence (cited): In Sept 2022 publicly defended the Space Coast Pride permit — which included a drag queen story time component — against State Rep. Randy Fine's demand the city pull the permit, posting on Facebook that drag queen story time 'is an event that happens nationwide and is attended by both adults and children' and that the organizers 'had as much right to organize as any.' This is affirmative defense of city-permitted LGBT promotion in public policy against a same-party state-rep challenge — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE. In Feb 2022 personally led Melbourne's 'Just Say No' anti-panhandling public-safety campaign rolled out as a Mayor+MPD partnership ('Just say no' message + city plan); 15-year retired Melbourne PD officer (K-9, Street Crimes, Community Policing, FTO) whose LE identity is centerpiece of his official bio; in June 2020 publicly defended the office of LE while distancing from a single FOP-president-personal-speech incident ('whoever put this up... they need to go') — affirmative pro-LE public-safety advocacy under the same standard applied to Vice Mayor Neuman's Jan 2025 MPD-partnership homelessness resolution → public_justice q0 TRUE. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite his '10-year low millage' boast: the question is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not generic low-tax claims. In Sept 2025 personally proposed renaming a stretch of Cypress Avenue to 'Charlie Kirk Lane' after Kirk's assassination ('He will not be forgotten in Melbourne!') and promoted 12+ Brevard County memorial billboards; council voted 5-1 against (only Vice Mayor Neuman supported). This is ideological signal but does not cleanly map to a christian_liberty rubric cell (Kirk Lane is a memorial naming of a Christian-affiliated public figure, not a Christian symbol or prayer policy) → christian_liberty cells null per the no-overread rule. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, transgender ideology, family/parental rights/schools (city does not run schools), invocation/Nativity policy, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, sanctuary/ICE cooperation, or 2A — those cells null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. DEVIATION ALERT (R out-of-line on biblical_marriage q4).

4.note: Publicly defended the Space Coast Pride permit — which included a drag queen story time — against State Rep. Randy Fine's demand the city pull it; wrote on Facebook that drag queen story time 'happens nationwide and is attended by both adults and children' and accused Fine of trying to 'inflame our community.'

0.note: Personally led the Feb 2022 'Just Say No' anti-panhandling public-safety campaign as a Mayor+MPD partnership rollout; 15-yr retired Melbourne PD officer (K-9, Street Crimes, Community Policing, FTO) whose LE identity is centerpiece of his official mayoral bio — affirmative pro-LE/public-safety advocacy under the same standard applied to Vice Mayor Neuman's MPD-partnership emergency resolution.

set.office: City Council, District 1, City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · term to Nov 2028)

profile.background: Retired U.S. Air Force Lt. Col. (30 years combined service incl. enlisted U.S. Navy, retired 2021); financial representative, Sky Peak Financial / New York Life; owner, Tax Time Solutions LLC since 2005. B.S. Electrical Engineering, Georgia Tech; MBA, Webster University (Patrick AFB). Melbourne Planning & Zoning Board chair 2021–2024; American Legion Post 191 1st Vice Commander; Kappa Alpha Psi alumnus. Elected November 5, 2024 with ~52% in nonpartisan general election for District 1; term ends November 2028. Race officially non-partisan; no documented personal partisan registration found this pass.

marcus-smith.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting District 1 Council Member, term to Nov 2028 (first elected Nov 5, 2024 with ~52% in nonpartisan general). Background: retired USAF Lt. Col. (30 yrs); GA Tech BSEE + Webster MBA; small-business owner; American Legion Post 191 1st VC. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on any LOCAL rubric area after social-media-heavy sweep (campaign site issues page, Ballotpedia, Space Coast Daily candidate profile, post-election bio site, VOTE411, Krimson PAC bio): no public statements on abortion, marriage/Pride, parental rights/schools, invocation/religious displays, deficit/BBA, election integrity, affirmative pro-LE advocacy (HIGH BAR), state-overreach, sanctuary/ICE, or 2A. SOFT-only directional signal: endorsed by Krimson PAC (self-described progressive PAC supporting 'underserved communities') — directional, not single-issue, does not clear HARD bar for any specific cell. Confidence: roster_verified (real seated official, zero policy positions findable). All LOCAL cells null pending future research or self-stated positions. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND.

set.office: City Council, District 2, City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · term to Nov 2026; previously D2 council 2004–2012; current tenure since 2018)

profile.background: Owner/founder, Paper & Plastics Direct, LLC (Melbourne foodservice-disposables distributor, est. 2010); prior national sales manager for an international foodservice-disposables company. A.A., Eastern Florida State College (formerly Brevard CC). Married 38+ years; one daughter; two grandchildren; four generations of family in Melbourne. First elected D2 2004; re-elected 2008; out 2012; returned Nov 6, 2018; re-elected Nov 8, 2022 with 68.67% (18,104–8,261). Vice Mayor Nov 2022–Nov 2023. Current term ends Nov 2026.

mark-larusso.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting District 2 council member, current term to Nov 2026 (first elected 2004, returned Nov 2018, re-elected 2022 with 68.67%). Owner of Paper & Plastics Direct LLC. Documented votes/positions found but NONE map cleanly to LOCAL rubric cells per FXBG high-bar discipline: (a) Sept 2025 voted AYE on FY2025-26 $315.4M budget with operating millage 7.0112 (11.4% above rollback rate, a tax INCREASE under FL TRIM) — economic_stewardship q2 is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not millage; higher millage may even improve budget balance, so null per discipline; (b) Sept 2024 voted AGAINST Mayor Alfrey's 'Charlie Kirk Lane' renaming proposal citing business optics ('what kind of community they are going to move their thousands of people to') — does not map cleanly to christian_liberty rubric cells, null; (c) Sept 2023 pushed back on Florida's Voice 'sponsored' wording re: Space Coast Pride event (semantic correction, not substantive content stance) — null on biblical_marriage; (d) 2019 Jan + Nov lone NO votes on continuing water fluoridation cited personal thyroid condition + physician's advice (not framed as anti-state-mandate) — null on refuse_state_overreach; (e) 2012 FOP Lodge #37 endorsement during a prior mayoral bid is too old to clear the FXBG affirmative-current-advocacy bar for public_justice q0 → null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, transgender ideology, parental rights/schools, invocation/Nativity policy, election integrity, sanctuary/ICE, or 2A. Confidence: roster_verified — real sitting official, votes documented but no LOCAL rubric cell scoreable above the high bar. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND.

set.office: City Council Member, District 3, City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · former 2024-25 Vice Mayor · term to Nov 2028)

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (active parishioner, Our Lady of Lourdes Church, Melbourne; younger brothers attend Ascension Catholic School)
profile.background: Director of Development, Senior Resource Association (Vero Beach), nonprofit fundraiser raising $5M+ for groups including Prevent Child Abuse America; former elected City Clerk, Oak Forest, IL. B.A. Political Science/Communications, Florida State University; M.A. Political Management, George Washington University. Chairman, Florida Federation of Young Republicans (2022–2023); President, Space Coast Young Republicans; Chief of Staff, Young Republican National Federation. Ran in 2024 R primary for Brevard County Commission Dist. 5 vs. Thad Altman. Prior local service: Brevard County Charter Review Commission; County Zoning Board of Adjustments; Melbourne Airport Authority Board; Space Coast TPO alternate. Elected Melbourne City Council D3 Nov 5, 2024 with 59% over Kendra Rae Thompson; served as ceremonial Vice Mayor 2024–2025; term ends Nov 2028.

david-neuman.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting District 3 council member (term to Nov 2028); served as ceremonial Vice Mayor 2024–2025. Party R: HARD evidence — past Florida Federation of Young Republicans chairman, Space Coast YR president, YRNF Chief of Staff, ran in 2024 R primary for Brevard County Commission D5 (race itself is non-partisan). Religion: Roman Catholic (Our Lady of Lourdes parishioner). HARD evidence (cited): In Sept 2025, with Mayor Alfrey, was one of only TWO YES votes for renaming Cypress Avenue 'Charlie Kirk Lane' after Kirk's assassination; on the dais publicly framed Kirk as 'a young leader … who believed in sharing the Gospel, being involved in your community, and being a family oriented person' and described the rename as 'a great way to promote free speech' — affirmative public-square testimony for the right to publicly profess Christ in public office and public life → christian_liberty q0 TRUE. In Jan 2025 sponsored a Resolution Declaring State of Emergency on Homelessness citing partnership with Melbourne Police Department: 'The City of Melbourne must lead on the issue of homelessness so that our residents can have a safe place to live work and play'; campaign-page commitment to 'partnering with Melbourne Police Department to ensure officers have resources' — affirmative pro-LE advocacy clearing the FXBG high bar → public_justice q0 TRUE. NOT scored on border_immigration despite Melbourne's March 25, 2025 287(g) MOA with ICE 'approved without debate': no individual roll-call citation for Neuman located in available reporting; per no-inference rule, leave null pending minutes pull. NOT scored on christian_liberty q3 (public-square Christian symbols / prayer in public bodies): the Kirk Lane vote honors a Christian-affiliated public figure but is not itself a Christian symbol or prayer policy — leave null per no-overread rule. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite campaign 'fiscal accountability' rhetoric: generic, narrow rubric (deficit/BBA), and individual roll-call on FY25-26 millage not located → null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, biblical marriage votes, transgender ideology, parental rights/CRT/SOGI, invocation/Nativity policy, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, or 2A — those cells null. Note: target of alleged death threats from former Councilwoman Yvonne Minus after the Kirk vote; council voted 5-2 to censure her.

0.note: One of two YES votes for the Charlie Kirk Lane renaming; on the dais described Kirk as 'a young leader … who believed in sharing the Gospel, being involved in your community, and being a family oriented person' and called the rename 'a great way to promote free speech' — affirmative public-square testimony for the right to publicly profess Christ.

Sponsored Jan 2025 Resolution Declaring State of Emergency on Homelessness with explicit partnership with Melbourne Police Department ('safe place to live work and play'); campaign-page commitment to 'partnering with Melbourne Police Department to ensure officers have resources' — affirmative pro-LE advocacy beyond routine governance.

set.office: City Council, District 4, City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting · term to Nov 2026)

profile.background: Florida-certified Realtor (Melbourne Realty, Inc.); Florida Private Investigator and Security certification; Certified Process Server & Notary (18th Judicial Circuit). Prior career: Brevard County Sheriff's Office dispatcher. Sociology major (concentration Forensic Psychology), Massachusetts College of Liberal Arts. Born North Adams, MA; moved to Florida 2012. Lives in Melbourne with fiancé and two children (per 2022 candidate profile). Lifetime NRA member; member Florida Association for Private Investigators; member Space Coast Realtors Association; member South Brevard NAACP (investigator on Legal Redress team). Elected Nov 8, 2022 to District 4 (officially non-partisan, four-year term); current term ends Nov 2026.

rachael-bassett.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting District 4 council member; first elected Nov 8, 2022; current term ends Nov 2026 (re-candidacy not confirmed in available votebrevard.gov listing as of search date). Background: former Brevard County Sheriff's Office dispatcher → current Realtor, FL Private Investigator, Certified Process Server (18th Judicial Circuit), Notary. HARD evidence (cited): 2022 endorsement by Brevard County Sheriff Wayne Ivey + sustained law-enforcement professional background (BCSO dispatcher; PI working civil/criminal cases for attorneys) prominently featured on official city bio + voted aye on FY26 budget that included dedicated debt-service millage for new Police HQ — affirmative pro-LE advocacy clearing the FXBG high bar (Sheriff's personal endorsement is functionally a single-issue LE-equivalent endorsement) → public_justice q0 TRUE. Lifetime NRA membership documented on official city bio — self-affiliation with the NRA's positions covering exactly q1 (red-flag laws, magazine limits, AWB, registries); per the skill's HARD-endorsement rule for single-issue groups, lifetime membership clears the bar → self_defense q1 TRUE. NOT scored on self_defense q0 (constitutional carry / no-permit): NRA membership doesn't directly evidence the narrower permit-less-carry position → null. NOT scored on economic_stewardship q2 despite aye vote on FY2025-26 budget with 11.4%-above-rollback millage: q2 is narrowly about deficit spending / BBA, not millage votes; higher millage may even improve budget balance → null per discipline. NOT scored on christian_liberty for her vote AGAINST Charlie Kirk Lane (Sept 2025): Kirk Lane isn't a Christian symbol per se and her stated rationale was not anti-Christian → null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life, biblical marriage, transgender ideology, parental rights/CRT/SOGI, invocation/Nativity, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, or sanctuary/ICE — those cells null. Religion / church: NO EVIDENCE FOUND. Partisan registration: not publicly confirmed (office officially non-partisan).

0.note: Endorsed by Brevard County Sheriff Wayne Ivey in 2022; LE professional background (former BCSO dispatcher, current PI) is centerpiece of official city bio; voted aye on FY2025-26 budget that included dedicated debt-service millage for the new Police Headquarters — affirmative pro-LE posture clearing the FXBG affirmative-advocacy bar.

1.note: Lifetime NRA member, publicly stated on her official city of Melbourne biography page — self-affiliation with the NRA's HARD positions opposing red-flag laws, magazine limits, AWB, and gun registries (the scope of q1).
